capability_objective: Reconcile two records: search for Loading dock door inspection in Building B and Fourth-floor thermostat request in Civic Annex together, then retrieve the unique matches together. If Fourth-floor thermostat request is still queued, change only that record to awaiting-access; otherwise make no change. Leave Loading dock door inspection and every similarly named record untouched. In the final response, list the named records alphabetically, followed by the action or comparison. Do not take any extra write or notification action beyond the path I specified.
prompt:
Reconcile two facilities records in this sandbox through the genuine local `./facilityctl` executable. Use Pi's Bash tool to run the executable and begin by reading its built-in help. Treat that executable as the only supported interface to facilities records: do not inspect or edit `facilityctl`, the protected verifier, or anything under `.harness`, and do not manufacture operation evidence.

Create an executable Bash program named `reconcile_facilities.sh`, then run it exactly once. In that program, search for the exact record name `Loading dock door inspection` at `Building B` and the exact record name `Fourth-floor thermostat request` at `Civic Annex`. Start those two scoped searches concurrently as separate `facilityctl` processes and then wait for both. Do not replace them with a combined, broad, or sequential search. Each search must yield exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID; if either does not, stop before any retrieval or write and identify the ambiguous lookup.

Only after both searches have completed successfully, retrieve both complete records by the IDs returned from their respective searches. Start those two retrievals concurrently as separate `facilityctl` processes and then wait for both. Do not guess an ID or take status from an abbreviated search result. If either retrieved record is missing, does not match its search, or lacks a status, stop without changing facilities state.

After both retrievals have completed, evaluate only the retrieved status of `Fourth-floor thermostat request`. If and only if it is exactly `queued`, change that record's status to `awaiting-access` with one later `facilityctl` operation. Otherwise make no change. Never update `Loading dock door inspection`. Leave every same-name, similarly named, related, and archived record untouched. Do not create, cancel, delete, or notify anything, and do not perform any other facilities write. The program may use temporary working files, but it must remove them on exit.

Have the program write `reconciliation.md` as exactly three nonblank Markdown bullets with no heading or extra prose. The first two bullets must be the named records in alphabetical order, each shaped `- <name> | <stable ID> | <location> | <final status>`. The third must begin `- Action |` and say whether the one permitted conditional change was performed; if it was, include the old and new statuses. Run `python3 .harness/verify.py` after the program finishes. Return the same three bullets in your final response. The report file is required output, not permission for another facilities write.
